Title: The clarification of two hydroxylation steps in the conversion of cholesterol to OSW-1 in Ornithogalum saundersiae

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Ornithogalum saundersiae; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ornithogalum saundersiae; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL-inducible promoter in yeast (pESC-Leu); constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana transient expression (pEAQ-HT)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early sterol oxidation module from cholesterol toward 16β-hydroxy-22-oxocholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP90B subfamily; conserved P450 motifs AGXETS, EXXR, FXXGXRXCXG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots, flowers, bulbs and leaves; undetectable in scape; ranked root > flower > bulb > leaf > scape
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In O. saundersiae: roots, flowers, bulbs and leaves, absent/trace in scape
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context with contrasting OSW-1 accumulation; galactose induction in yeast; cholesterol feeding in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Highest in bulb; absent/trace in scape
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes OSW-1 biosynthesis by initiating cholesterol oxidation and supplying 22R-hydroxycholesterol for downstream conversion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP90B94 was identified from comparative transcriptomics as a CYP90B candidate correlated with OSW-1-producing tissues. 2. It catalyses cholesterol specifically to 22R-hydroxycholesterol in yeast, microsomes and N. benthamiana. 3. Together with OsCYP90G11, it enables the pathway from cholesterol to 16β-hydroxy-22-oxo-cholesterol, an OSW-1 precursor. 4. It also accepts 7β-hydroxycholesterol, indicating substrate promiscuity and likely roles beyond OSW-1 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome mining; RT-PCR cloning; phylogenetic analysis; yeast co-expression with OsCPR2; GC-MS; HPLC; NMR structural identification; microsome enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RT-qPCR; sqRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cholesterol at C-22 with R stereospecificity to form 22R-hydroxycholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed early access step into cholesterol-derived steroid metabolism; initiates OSW-1 branch according to this study
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity suggests activity in additional sterol-derived pathways, which may complicate pathway-specific engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing OSW-1 early pathway in heterologous hosts; should be stacked with OsCYP90G11 and later tailoring enzymes for OSW-1 production; pathway specificity may need optimization because of promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis of bulb vs scape; data availability upon request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 22R-specific rather than BR-type mixed 22R/22S hydroxylation; not proportional to OSW-1 levels across all tissues, implying additional pathway roles; no direct subcellular localization experiment reported

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Ornithogalum saundersiae; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ornithogalum saundersiae; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL-inducible promoter in yeast (pESC-Leu); constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana transient expression (pEAQ-HT)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early sterol oxidation module from cholesterol toward 16β-hydroxy-22-oxocholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: multifunctional sterol hydroxylase/oxidase acting at C16 and C22
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP90G subfamily; conserved P450 motifs AGXETS, EXXR, FXXGXRXCXG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flowers, roots, bulbs and leaves; undetectable in scape; highest in flowers followed by roots, bulbs, leaves and scapes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In O. saundersiae: flowers, roots, bulbs and leaves, absent/trace in scape
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context with contrasting OSW-1 accumulation; galactose induction in yeast; cholesterol or hydroxycholesterol substrate feeding in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Highest in bulb; absent/trace in scape
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes OSW-1 biosynthesis by converting 22R-hydroxycholesterol to 16β,22R-dihydroxycholesterol and then to 16β-hydroxy-22-oxocholesterol; also directly converts cholesterol to 16β,22S-dihydroxycholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP90G11 was identified from comparative transcriptomics as a CYP90G candidate associated with OSW-1-producing tissues. 2. It converts cholesterol to 16β,22S-dihydroxycholesterol in yeast, microsomes and N. benthamiana. 3. In combination with OsCYP90B94, it accepts 22R-hydroxycholesterol and forms 16β,22R-dihydroxycholesterol, then further oxidizes this intermediate to 16β-hydroxy-22-oxocholesterol. 4. Its broad substrate promiscuity suggests functions beyond OSW-1 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome mining; RT-PCR cloning; phylogenetic analysis; yeast co-expression with OsCPR2 and with OsCYP90B94; GC-MS; LC-MS2; NMR structural identification; microsome enzyme assays with cholesterol, 22R-hydroxycholesterol and dihydroxycholesterols; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RT-qPCR; sqRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses multiple consecutive oxidations in sterol modification: hydroxylates cholesterol to 16β,22S-dihydroxycholesterol; hydroxylates 22R-hydroxycholesterol at C16 to form 16β,22R-dihydroxycholesterol; oxidizes the C22 hydroxyl of 16β,22R-dihydroxycholesterol to a 22-oxo group forming 16β-hydroxy-22-oxocholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream to midstream; consecutive oxidation steps after pathway initiation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor of downstream OSW-1 intermediate pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early pathway enzyme controlling entry/flux together with OsCYP90B94, but not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsCPR2; functional coupling with OsCYP90B94 in pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous activity on multiple sterol substrates may divert flux or create by-products in engineering hosts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for reconstructing early OSW-1 biosynthesis; should be combined with OsCYP90B94 and downstream oxygenases/glycosyltransferases/acyltransferases; catalytic promiscuity may require host/pathway optimization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis of bulb vs scape; data availability upon request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Displays a combination of activities unlike homologues: steroid C22 oxidase, C16-hydroxylase, and 16β,22S-hydroxylase. It does not convert 16β,22S-dihydroxycholesterol onward to the 22-oxo product, but does convert the 22R epimer.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Redox support for early sterol oxidation module
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 reductase class II; membrane-anchor domain, P450-binding domain, FMN-binding domains, FAD-binding domains, NADPH-binding domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues including scape
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In O. saundersiae, ubiquitous across roots, bulbs, leaves, flowers and scape
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast functional assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redox partner enabling OsCYP90B94 and OsCYP90G11 catalytic activity, thereby supporting OSW-1 precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPR2 was identified as the only differentially expressed CPR candidate from transcriptome analysis. 2. Co-expression with OsCYP90B94 enabled cholesterol 22R-hydroxylation, whereas OsCYP90B94 alone was inactive in yeast. 3. OsCPR2 is therefore the functional NADPH-cytochrome P450 oxidoreductase partner for OSW-1-related P450s. 4. Its expression in all tissues indicates it participates in multiple pathways beyond OSW-1 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome mining; phylogenetic analysis; RT-PCR cloning; yeast co-expression dependency assays with OsCYP90B94 and OsCYP90G11; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: NADPH and partner CYP450 redox cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from NADPH via FAD/FMN to partner cytochrome P450 enzymes to support monooxygenase catalysis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for enzymatic activity of target-product-forming P450s
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad interaction with multiple P450s may affect other pathways if overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary auxiliary component for heterologous reconstruction of OSW-1 early pathway with P450 enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis of bulb vs scape; data availability upon request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Class II CPR; not OSW-1-specific because expressed in scape and likely supports multiple P450s
